summary.description: Directory rename refactor with no functional code changes. No new code implementation, no new dependencies, no configuration changes affecting security.
key_findings: ✅ No hardcoded secrets detected in modified files
✅ No new dependencies introduced (directory rename only)

owasp_findings.a1_injection: PASS - No SQL injection, NoSQL injection, or OS command injection detected
owasp_findings.a3_sensitive_data_exposure: PASS - No sensitive data handling changes
owasp_findings.a5_broken_access_control: PASS - No access control logic changes
owasp_findings.a7_xss: PASS - No user input processing changes

dependency_scan.description: STORY-440 is a directory rename refactor with no new package additions. Existing dependency vulnerabilities (lodash, tmp) are pre-existing and outside scope of this story.

authentication_review.description: No authentication or authorization code was added or modified in this refactor

security_relevant_files.analysis: Shell script performs file copying and placeholder substitution. Uses 'set -e' for error handling. No unsafe commands detected (no eval, exec, or unquoted variables in critical paths). Safe use of sed with date variables.

Python script contains regex patterns for security anti-pattern detection (hardcoded passwords, secrets, connection strings). These are detection patterns only, not exploits. No actual secrets found in the code.

risk_assessment.technical_risk: NONE - Directory rename only, no functional changes

pass_fail_criteria.dependency_vulnerabilities: PASS (no new dependencies - pre-existing vulns not in scope)

recommendations.action: Run 'npm audit fix' to address pre-existing vulnerabilities at project level (outside STORY-440 scope)

scope_notes.reason_for_pass: STORY-440 contains no new code, no new dependencies, and no security-sensitive changes. It is a pure directory rename refactor applying ADR-017 naming conventions to existing files.

methodology: OWASP Top 10 pattern scanning, hardcoded secret detection, dependency vulnerability scanning, shell script safety analysis
